author: barnabasbusa
content:
No — Marius is mistaken. bal-devnet-6 does not have the forkchoice fix.

Here's the proof:

- The fix is geth PR #34767 ("eth/catalyst: allow reorging the head block to a parent"), commit 4d2af275e, authored by Marius himself on 2026-05-06. The commit message even says: "Implements https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/786/changes" — so yes, your earlier instinct was right: #786 ↔ #34767.
- bal-devnet-6 was branched from master at b70d9a4b8 on 2026-04-24 — about 12 days before the fix landed.
- git merge-base --is-ancestor 4d2af275e b70d9a4b8 returns false, confirming the fix isn't in the branch's history.
- And the actual diff git diff origin/master..origin/bal-devnet-6 -- eth/catalyst/api.go shows the new logic (finalized check, maxReorgDepth, TooDeepReorg) being removed going from master → bal-devnet-6. On the branch, L323-327 still unconditionally logs "Ignoring beacon update to old head" and returns VALID — the exact pre-#786 behavior.

So if bal-devnet-6 is what's running on glamsterdam-devnet-3, the EL there will still no-op on any forkchoice update pointing at an ancestor of current head, which is exactly the bug #786 was written to fix.

Probably worth pinging Marius back — either a different branch needs to be deployed, or 4d2af275e needs to be cherry-picked onto bal-devnet-6.
